name: slack
tagline: ClawHub (160★): Use when you need to control Slack from Clawdbot via the slack tool, including reacting to messages or pinning/unpinning it
verdict: Across all ten supported actions, there's total silence on failure — invalid IDs, permission errors, rate limits, and bad tokens all go unaddressed, so problems surface as confusing breakage rather than a clear error. No version tracking, and no stated boundary against other messaging tools.

highlights.summary: Across all ten actions (invalid IDs, permission errors, missing message, rate limits, unauthenticated bot token) there is no mention anywhere of what to do on failure — total silen
No 'NOT for' / out-of-scope / pointer-to-other-skill language anywhere in description or body, despite the skill exposing ~10 distinct actions where misfire against adjacent messag
No version field in frontmatter, no changelog section, and no dated revision notes anywhere in the file.
